t1.quote: Before Freedoliapp I had 4 spreadsheets and 3 WhatsApp groups to manage a single PO. Now everything is in one place and I actually trust the numbers.
t1.name: Marta S.

t2.quote: The PO PDF generation alone saved us hours every week. But what really changed things was having supplier terms, samples and shipments all linked together.
t2.name: Jordi P.

t3.quote: I can finally see my real margin per product after Amazon fees, freight and customs. The profit view is something I could never build properly in Excel.
t3.name: Laura M.

q1.a: Yes — all plans include a 14-day free trial. No credit card required.

q2.q: Can I switch plans at any time?
q2.a: Yes, upgrades and downgrades take effect immediately. You will be charged or credited on a prorated basis.

q3.q: Does it work with multiple Amazon marketplaces?
q3.a: Yes. Connect ES, DE, FR, IT, UK, US and more to a single workspace. Data is unified across all marketplaces.

q4.q: What do I need to connect Amazon SP-API?
q4.a: A Seller Central account with API access. Setup takes about 5 minutes through the guided wizard. Available on Growth and Scale plans.

q5.a: Data is stored on Supabase (EU region, ISO 27001 infrastructure). All connections are encrypted and row-level security is enforced per organisation.

dashboard.createProject: Create your first product to start the launch pipeline.
dashboard.openOrCreatePO: You have products; create a purchase order or open a project to move forward.

assistant.title: Assistant

answers.what_can_i_do: This screen lets you navigate and act by context: create or open projects, orders, review phases and viability. Use the sidebar buttons or each section's actions.
answers.next_step: The next step depends on your state: if you have no projects, create one; if you do, open orders or review activity. Dashboard and each screen show a recommendation.
answers.where_orders: Purchase orders (POs) are in the Orders section. Access from the sidebar or Dashboard.
answers.where_viability: Viability and margin are inside each project, in the viability/profitability tab. Open a project and look for «Viability» or «Margin».
answers.where_projects: Projects are in the Projects section. Access from the sidebar or Dashboard.
answers.what_means_state: PO statuses (draft, confirmed, shipped, etc.) and terms like FNSKU are in the help manual. Open «Help» in the top bar and search for «Orders» or «Amazon readiness».
answers.flow_project_po: The flow is: (1) Create a project in Projects, (2) Complete phases (viability, readiness), (3) Create a PO in Orders and link it to the project, (4) Track inventory and shipments. You can go to Projects or Orders from here.
answers.po_blocked: If a PO is blocked, check: order status, product Amazon readiness, pending tasks, and shipments. Open Orders and the PO detail to see alerts and tasks.
answers.what_missing_phase: To advance a phase you need to complete the current phase gates: product data, viability, Amazon readiness, tasks. The project view shows what's pending.

assistant.noMatch: No exact answer found. Try «What can I do here?» or «What's the next step?», or open the help manual.

idea.title: Idea & research
items: Clarify the product idea and target customer.
Collect 3–5 reference products on Amazon.
List key differentiators you want to test.

viability.title: Viability
items: Estimate landed cost per unit.
Check expected Amazon fees and margins.
Validate minimum order quantity vs. budget.

quotes.title: Supplier quotes
items: Request quotes from at least 3 suppliers.
Compare lead times, MOQs and payment terms.
Shortlist the 1–2 best options.

samples.title: Samples
items: Order samples from shortlisted suppliers.
Review quality, packaging and defects.
Decide which supplier you want to go with.

po.title: Purchase order
items: Confirm final specs and packaging.
Negotiate final price and payment terms.
Send and confirm the purchase order (PO).

shipment.title: Shipment
items: Choose Incoterms and shipping method.
Plan labels and carton packing.
Book freight and share dates with supplier.

amazon.title: Send to Amazon / go live
items: Create or update the Amazon listing.
Create the inbound shipment in Seller Central.
Monitor check-in and first sales.

helpAssistant.title: Help
helpAssistant.noResults: No results. Try contacting us instead.

firstProduct.q: How do I add my first product?
firstProduct.a: Go to Projects and click "New project". Add a name, an ASIN if you have one, and the phases will fill in as you load data.

kpis.a: The main KPIs are monthly revenue, net margin, expenses, and active orders. Each card shows the change vs. the previous month.

amazon.q: How do I connect my Amazon account?
amazon.a: In Settings → Connections, click "Connect Amazon". You'll be redirected to Seller Central to authorize read-only OAuth access.

export.q: How do I export from Sellerboard?
export.a: In Sellerboard, go to "Reports" and export the "Dashboard" or "Products" report as CSV. Then upload it here — we'll auto-detect the columns.

formats.q: Which file formats do you accept?
formats.a: CSV, TSV, XLSX, XLS and JSON. We auto-detect the separator and number format (1,234.56 vs 1.234,56).

excel.q: Can I import from my own Excel?
excel.a: Yes. Pick "Generic Excel / CSV" as the source and we'll let you map each column manually to the matching field (revenue, COGS, etc.).

duplicates.q: What happens if I import duplicate data?
duplicates.a: We deduplicate by ASIN + date. Re-imports update existing values instead of creating new rows.

asin.a: ASIN (Amazon Standard Identification Number) is the 10-character code starting with B0 that uniquely identifies a product on Amazon.

suppliers.a: We query Alibaba, 1688 and Zentrada in parallel using keywords from the title or your description. If you have Helium 10 or Jungle Scout connected, we query those too.

cost.q: How much does an AI analysis cost?
cost.a: Each plan has a free monthly quota (Starter: 5, Growth: 50, Scale: unlimited). If you exceed it, connect your own AI key for unlimited analyses.

byok.q: Can I use my own AI account?
byok.a: Yes. In Settings → Power up your AI you can connect Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Mistral, Groq or Ollama. When connected, it doesn't consume your quota.

plan.q: How do I change my plan?
plan.a: In Settings → Billing you can see your current plan and upgrade/downgrade. The change applies immediately.

amazon.a: In Settings → Connections click "Connect Amazon" and authorize from Seller Central. Connection is read-only and you can revoke it any time.

aiKey.q: Where do I find my AI key?
aiKey.a: Depends on the provider: Anthropic at console.anthropic.com → Settings → API Keys; OpenAI at platform.openai.com → API keys. The connection wizard has direct links.

help.a: Each activation step has explanatory text. If you have doubts about what to enter, use the contact button below and we'll walk you through it.

skip.a: Optional steps have a "Skip" button below. Required ones (company name, marketplace) must be completed to access the app.
